title: Korean Gyeran Mari (Rolled Omelette)

subs.name: a chickpea-flour (besan) omelette roll as a vegan alternative — whisk 4 tbsp besan with water to a thin batter and roll the same way
finely chopped coriander leaves for a more Indian-inflected flavour
finely diced capsicum or corn kernels — same sweetness and colour effect

steps.text: Crack the eggs into a bowl and beat well with salt until completely combined — no streaks of white should remain. Stir in the finely chopped spring onion, grated carrot and green chilli if using. The mixture should be a pale yellow flecked with colour.
Heat a tawa or non-stick frying pan over medium-low flame — this low heat is important so each layer sets slowly without browning too fast. Brush a thin, even layer of oil across the entire surface.

Pour about one-third of the egg mixture into the pan and tilt to spread into a thin, even layer. Let it set on the bottom until the surface is still just slightly wet on top but firm enough to hold a roll, about 90 seconds.
Starting from the far edge, use a spatula to gently roll the omelette towards you, forming a cylinder. Push the roll to the far end of the pan. Oil the empty space lightly.

Pour another third of the egg mixture into the empty space, letting a little run under the existing roll to bond them. Once the new layer has just set at the bottom, roll the existing log over it and push it back to the far end. Repeat with the remaining egg.

Once the full roll is firm, golden and evenly shaped, transfer to a cutting board. Rest for 1 minute, then cut crosswise into 2 cm rounds with a sharp knife. The cross-section should reveal golden and pale yellow rings. Serve warm or at room temperature.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: carrot → finely diced capsicum or corn kernels — same sweetness and colour effect; spring-onion → finely chopped coriander leaves for a more Indian-inflected flavour; egg → a chickpea-flour (besan) omelette roll as a vegan alternative — whisk 4 tbsp besan with water to a thin batter and roll the same way
indian_kitchen_adaptation: A regular round tawa works perfectly — the roll will be slightly irregular in shape but just as delicious. A non-stick frying pan makes rolling easier. The key is medium-low heat; high heat browns the exterior before the inside has set. Spring onion (hara pyaz), carrot and green chilli are all standard Indian pantry items. These rolled omelette rounds travel well in a lunchbox and can be prepared the night before.
